titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5977

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=752 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:57:31.581822+00 | translation moa=753 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:57:31.598434+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus ad quos presens Scriptum peruenerit  Patricius filius Helye Dunning’ de Renigt’ Salutem . Nouerit vniuersitas uestra me quietum clamasse
domui de Coldingham de Me et heredibus meis In perpetuum homagium et seruicium quod mihi debebant henricus frater meus et heredes sui pro vno
tofto et uno crofto unius acre in uilla de Rengt’ . et duabus acris in campo eiusdem uille . scilicet . in occidentali parte de cromstaniside unam acram .
et magnam acram super aletone . cum omnibus eschaetis que mihi et heredibus meis de dicta terra possent accidere . Dabunt etiam dictus henricus et heredes
sui singulis annis nomine firme domui de Coldingham Sex denarios ad pascha . Et dabunt per Marcheto duodecim denarios . pro herieto duo
decim denarios . pro forisfacto duodecim denarios . Et In testimonium huius mee quiete clamationis sigillum meum huic scripto apposui . Testibus
Domino Willelmo de Morhint’ . Domino alano de Swint’ . Ada de prendergest . Roberto . andr’ . alex’ . de paxt’ . ada de Reston’ . Bertramo filio eius
Mauricio de aytona . Roberto filio Reginaldi . Radulfo preposito . Rogero filio eius . Roberto filio gregorii . Iohanne filio Helye de aytona . Et aliis

To all Christ's faithful to whom the present written document shall come, Patrick son of Elias Dunning of Renton sends greeting. Know me all of you to have quitclaimed to the house of Coldingham for myself and my heirs in perpetuity the homage and service that Henry my brother and his heirs owe me for one toft and one croft of one acre in the toun of Renton, and two acres in the plain of the same toun, that is, one acre on the west side of 'Cromstaniside', and a large acre above 'Aletone', with all escheats that could be due to me an dmy heirs for the said land. The said Henry and his heirs shall each year give as ferme to the house of Coldingham six pence at Easter; and they shall give twelve pence for merchet, twelve pence for heriot, twelve pence for forfeit. And as witness of this my quitclaim I have applied my seal to this written document. As witness, the lord William of Mordington, the lord Alan of Swinton, Adam of Prenderguest, Robert, Andrew, Alexander of Paxton, Adam of Reston, Bertram his son, Maurice of Ayton, Robert son of Reginald, Ralph the provost, Roger his son, Robert son of Gregory, John son of Elias of Ayton, and others.
